raw.objective: To provide “an additional insight into CBZ pharmacokinetics and determination of absolute bioavailability after administration of an oral suspension”
raw.covariates: Not possible due to only 2 subjects being analysed
raw.observations: In the case of rapid absorption formula of CBZ, a two-compartment model might have to be taken into account, in opposition to the usual one-compartment model used. Only two subjects were included, and thus the results are not necessarily useful for population predictions
raw.dose: Single oral dose and single iv infusion concurrently in two healthy subjects
raw.structure: 2 compartment, first order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: alpha=theta1 beta=theta2 CL=theta3 K10=theta4 K12=theta5 K21=theta6 V1=theta7 Vss=theta8
raw.parameter_values: Values reported for subject1/subject2: theta1=3.09/1.68 theta2=0.018/0.015 theta3=1.38/1.17 theta4=0.032/0.026 theta5=1.35/0.72 theta6=1.73/0.95 theta7=0.57/0.51 theta8=1.02/0.89

structural_model.description: 2 compartment, first order absorption and elimination

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Single oral dose and single iv infusion concurrently in two healthy subjects
study.objective: To provide “an additional insight into CBZ pharmacokinetics and determination of absolute bioavailability after administration of an oral suspension”

covariates.tested: Not possible due to only 2 subjects being analysed
covariates.raw: Not possible due to only 2 subjects being analysed

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
